commands.author: ChunkyPanda29

target.title: fix: classify HTTP 429 'temporarily overloaded' as overloaded, not rate_limit
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Some providers (Z.ai/GLM, Anthropic) return HTTP 429 with an "overloaded" message body when their model fleet is at capacity. This is a **transient server-side overload**, not a per-account rate limit.

OpenClaw was misclassifying these as `rate_limit`, triggering **immediate model fallback** instead of retry-with-backoff. This caused unnecessary failovers and ~30-60s stalls on every turn when the provider was overloaded.

### Root Cause

Two issues in `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts`:

**1. `classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage` — ordering bug**

The `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern includes `/429/` as a match. So a message like `"429 The service may be temporarily overloaded"` matched `rate_limit` before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` was ever evaluated.

**2. `classifyFailoverClassificationFromHttpStatus` — missing overloaded check**

The HTTP 429 handler always returned `rate_limit` for non-billing 429s. The 503 and 499 handlers already check `messageReason === "overloaded"` — the 429 handler should do the same.

### Fix

1. Reorder `classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage` to check `isOverloadedErrorMessage` **before** `isRateLimitErrorMessage`
2. Add `if (messageReason === "overloaded") return messageClassification;` to the 429 status handler

### Impact

- **Before:** 429 "temporarily overloaded" → `rate_limit` → immediate model fallback (~30-60s stall)
- **After:** 429 "temporarily overloaded" → `overloaded` → retry with backoff (same as 503 behavior)

## Evidence

### Screenshot (runtime classifier proof)

![Runtime Proof: 429 Overloaded Classification](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/ChunkyPanda29/0dd1e93d08e91135f2e85272953fef8d/raw/runtime-proof.png)

### Full test suite output (3 test files, 113 tests, all pass)

```
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts > HTTP 429 with overloaded message body > classifies 429 'temporarily overloaded' as overloaded, not rate_limit
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts > HTTP 429 with overloaded message body > classifies 429 'overloaded' (bare) as overloaded
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts > HTTP 429 with overloaded message body > classifies 429 'model is at capacity' as overloaded
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts > HTTP 429 with overloaded message body > still classifies 429 'rate limit exceeded' as rate_limit
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts > HTTP 429 with overloaded message body > still classifies 429 'too many requests' as rate_limit
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts > HTTP 429 with overloaded message body > still classifies 429 billing messages as billing

✓ src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > falls back on a Zhipu GLM 1305 overload body and classifies it as overloaded

✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts > handleAssistantFailover > rotate_profile branch > retries the same model before spending a rate-limit profile rotation
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts > handleAssistantFailover > rotate_profile branch > does not spend same-model retry budget on quota-style rate limits

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  113 passed (113)
   Duration  79.64s
```

### Runtime classifier proof (classifyFailoverSignal — the actual path OpenClaw uses)

```
PASS: Z.ai/GLM 429 overloaded (real-world, with status) -> overloaded
PASS: Z.ai/GLM 429 overloaded (message only, no explicit status) -> overloaded
PASS: Anthropic 429 overloaded -> overloaded
PASS: 503 overloaded (existing behavior, should be overloaded) -> overloaded
PASS: 429 rate limit (should stay rate_limit) -> rate_limit
PASS: 429 too many requests (should stay rate_limit) -> rate_limit
PASS: 429 billing (should stay billing) -> billing
```

Key observations:
- 429 "temporarily overloaded" now classifies as `overloaded` (not `rate_limit`)
- `classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind` returns `timeout` (retryable) for overloaded, vs `rate_limit` for actual rate limits — this is the existing behavior that was being bypassed
- 429 rate-limit and billing classifications are unchanged (regression-safe)
- 503 overloaded behavior is unchanged (parity confirmed)

### Real OpenClaw runtime path evidence (trajectory logs)

Redacted `model.fallback_step` trajectory events extracted from real OpenClaw session `.trajectory.jsonl` logs — the actual runtime failover path, not isolated classifier output.

#### Before-fix: June 24, 2026 — Heartbeat session (4 unnecessary failovers)

Z.ai/GLM returned HTTP 429 "temporarily overloaded" 4 times across two runs. Each was misclassified as `rate_limit`, triggering unnecessary model fallback:

```
# 2026-06-24T12:35:19Z — model.fallback_step
provider: zai | model: glm-5.1
fallbackStepFromModel: zai/glm-5.1 → fallbackStepToModel: zai/glm-5
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit          ← misclassified (should be overloaded)
fallbackStepFromFailureDetail: 429 The service may be temporarily overloaded, please try again later
fallbackStepChainPosition: 1 | fallbackStepFinalOutcome: next_fallback

# 2026-06-24T12:36:08Z
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit          ← misclassified
fallbackStepFromFailureDetail: 429 The service may be temporarily overloaded, please try again later
fallbackStepChainPosition: 2 | fallbackStepFinalOutcome: succeeded

# 2026-06-24T12:50:43Z (second occurrence)
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit          ← misclassified
fallbackStepFromFailureDetail: 429 The service may be temporarily overloaded, please try again later
fallbackStepChainPosition: 1 | fallbackStepFinalOutcome: next_fallback

# 2026-06-24T12:50:59Z
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit          ← misclassified
fallbackStepFromFailureDetail: 429 The service may be temporarily overloaded, please try again later
fallbackStepChainPosition: 2 | fallbackStepFinalOutcome: succeeded
```

#### Before-fix: June 25, 2026 — Subagent runs (4 chain exhaustions in 3 min)

During a Z.ai fleet-wide overload, 4 subagent runs all hit 429 overloaded. All misclassified as `rate_limit`, all resulted in **chain_exhausted**:

```
# 2026-06-25T08:31:19Z — subagent
fallbackStepFromModel: zai/glm-5.1
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit          ← misclassified
fallbackStepFromFailureDetail: 429 The service may be temporarily overloaded, please try again later
fallbackStepFinalOutcome: chain_exhausted

# 2026-06-25T08:31:53Z — subagent
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit | fallbackStepFinalOutcome: chain_exhausted

# 2026-06-25T08:32:51Z — subagent
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit | fallbackStepFinalOutcome: chain_exhausted

# 2026-06-25T08:33:56Z — subagent
fallbackStepFromFailureReason: rate_limit | fallbackStepFinalOutcome: chain_exhausted
```

With the fix, these classify as `overloaded` → retry-with-backoff on the same model, avoiding cascading chain exhaustion across fallback models (which were all on the same overloaded Z.ai fleet).

### Before-fix logs (real-world)

```
event=embedded_run_agent_end | zai/glm-5.1 | run=e600fd8a | isError=true
failoverReason=rate_limit
rawErrorPreview="429 The service may be temporarily overloaded, please try again later"
```

After fix, this same error classifies as `failoverReason=overloaded`, engaging retry-with-backoff instead of immediate fallback.

### New unit tests (6 in failover-matches.test.ts)

| Input | Expected | Status |
|-------|----------|--------|
| `"429 The service may be temporarily overloaded..."` | `overloaded` | New |
| `"429 overloaded"` | `overloaded` | New |
| `"429 The selected model is at capacity"` | `overloaded` | New |
| `"429 Rate limit exceeded"` | `rate_limit` | Regression |
| `"429 Too many requests"` | `rate_limit` | Regression |
| `'429 {"code":1311,...}'` (billing) | `billing` | Regression |

## Related Issues

- #93211 — Model fallback not triggered for Zhipu (GLM) error code 1305 (overloaded pattern mismatch) — closed, related Chinese-text overload pattern gap
- #85103 — Model fallback chain not triggered on provider-wide quota exhaustion — open, complementary fix for fallback chain escalation
- #51336 — Surface API provider name in error/overload messages — open, related UX improvement

commands.author: henryyuen168-ship-it

target.title: fix: project visible session transcripts
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Visible WhatsApp/group session surfaces were showing internal delivery artifacts as if they were user-visible chat history. On `agent:alfred:whatsapp:group:120363425559039020@g.us`, `sessions_history(includeTools=false)` exposed assistant tool-call-only rows and terminal source-delivery confirmations like `Sent the Branch Basics links in WhatsApp.` `sessions_list(includeLastMessage=true)` also used those confirmations as previews.

This PR keeps raw transcript records intact for replay/debugging and `includeTools=true`, while projecting a cleaner visible chat transcript for history/list/preview surfaces.

## Summary

- Add shared visible-chat transcript projection helpers.
- Hide tool-call/tool-result artifacts and terminal source-delivery confirmations from visible WA/group history and previews.
- De-duplicate `delivery-mirror` assistant rows only when an occurrence in the same user-delimited turn is replaced by a synthetic `openclawMessageToolMirror`.
- Preserve standalone `delivery-mirror` assistant rows, including repeated identical text such as `Done.`, so bounded history/list/preview windows do not lose delivered replies.
- Preserve raw transcript records and `includeTools=true`/debug paths.

## Redacted Behavior Proof

Full-context message-tool turn:

- Before visible projection: redacted user request, raw `provider=openclaw/model=delivery-mirror` delivered text, assistant `message` tool call, tool result, terminal `Sent ... in WhatsApp.` confirmation.
- After visible projection: redacted user request plus one synthetic `openclawMessageToolMirror` assistant row with the delivered text. Raw tool artifacts and terminal confirmation are hidden from visible chat; raw records remain available with `includeTools=true`.

Bounded-window standalone delivery mirror:

- Before this review fix: a window containing only a redacted user request plus `provider=openclaw/model=delivery-mirror` delivered text projected to the user request only, so `sessions_history`, hydrated `sessions_list` messages, and `lastMessagePreview` lost the delivered reply.
- After this review fix: the same bounded window preserves the standalone delivery mirror. The delivered text remains visible in `sessions_history(includeTools=false)`, `sessions_list(messageLimit=...)`, and last-message preview.

Repeated identical delivery text:

- Before the occurrence-aware fix: an earlier standalone `delivery-mirror` with text `Done.` could be hidden by a later unrelated synthetic message-tool mirror that also said `Done.`.
- After the occurrence-aware fix: suppression is scoped by user-delimited turn and occurrence, so the earlier standalone `Done.` remains a raw delivery mirror and the later replaced `Done.` becomes the synthetic mirror.

Terminal proof on `d1184d7c01`:

```json
[
  {"role":"user","text":"Send the first redacted update.","deliveryMirror":false,"syntheticMirror":false},
  {"role":"assistant","text":"Done.","deliveryMirror":true,"syntheticMirror":false},
  {"role":"user","text":"Send the second redacted update.","deliveryMirror":false,"syntheticMirror":false},
  {"role":"assistant","text":"Done.","deliveryMirror":false,"syntheticMirror":true}
]
```

## Evidence

Local verification on branch `fix/koro-whatsapp-message-tool-final` at `d1184d7c01`:

- RED before fix: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts` failed on repeated-identical history/list regressions because the first standalone `Done.` disappeared.
- GREEN after fix: same focused command passed: gateway shard 4 files / 380 tests; agents shard 2 files / 22 tests.
- Broader command passed: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` passed: gateway shard 6 files / 452 tests; auto-reply shard 2 files / 225 tests; agents shard 3 files / 57 tests.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo` exited clean.
- `git diff --check` exited clean.

## MC

- Implements MC task `js7chanc5ewxg2jwf6aqwbmdt989g9fj`.
- Keep MC open until this PR is merged to `origin/main` and verified there.

commands.author: wendy-chsy

target.title: fix(irc): prevent ghost nick collisions on rejoin after network delay
target.body:
### Summary

- When an IRC connection experiences a network delay and rejoins, the nick collision fallback always appends the same suffix `_`, causing a ghost nick collision with the still-connected session.
- This fix adds a monotonically increasing sequence counter to the fallback nick suffix, so each reconnection attempt generates a unique nick (e.g., `bot_`, `bot_2`, `bot_3`).
- Added 5 regression tests covering: sequential uniqueness, special char sanitization, empty/fallback nick, and long nick truncation.

### Linked issue

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/96064

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Repeated IRC reconnections after network delay fail because every fallback nick is identical (`bot_`). The module-level counter produces `bot_`, `bot_2`, `bot_3`, ... across reconnect calls.
- **Real environment tested:** ngircd 26 running locally on port 6667. Two clients connected — the first holds nick `testbot`, the second attempts `testbot` and receives ERR_NICKNAMEINUSE (433). `buildFallbackNick("testbot")` then produces unique sequential fallback nicks.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Started ngircd 26 locally: `ngircd -n -f /tmp/ngircd.conf`
  2. Real IRC proof: `node /tmp/irc-proof.mjs` — full end-to-end verification
  3. Unit tests: `pnpm test extensions/irc/src/client.test.ts` — 9 passed
  4. Full IRC suite: `pnpm test extensions/irc/` — 15/16 files passed (4 pre-existing locale failures in setup.test.ts unrelated to this change)
  5. Build: `pnpm build` — clean
- **Evidence after fix (real ngircd server):**
  ```
  Phase 1 — Establish ghost session holding nick 'testbot'
    ✅ Ghost connected with nick "testbot"

  Phase 2 — Second client tries nick 'testbot' (simulates rejoin after network delay)
    ✅ Got ERR_NICKNAMEINUSE (433): ":irc.test.local 433 * testbot :Nickname already in use"
    ✅ Ghost nick collision reproduced on real IRC server

  Phase 3 — Fallback nick counter prevents duplicate nicks
    call 1: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_"
    call 2: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_2"
    call 3: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_3"
    call 4: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_4"
    call 5: buildFallbackNick("testbot") → "testbot_5"
    ✅ All 5 nicks unique: testbot_, testbot_2, testbot_3, testbot_4, testbot_5

  Phase 4 — Connect to IRC server with each fallback nick
    ✅ "testbot_" → connected successfully
    ✅ "testbot_2" → connected successfully
    ✅ "testbot_3" → connected successfully

  Phase 5 — Edge case coverage
    ✅ sanitize whitespace + special chars: "my bot!" → "mybot_"
    ✅ all-special chars → openclaw fallback: "!!!" → "openclaw_2"
    ✅ empty string → openclaw fallback: "" → "openclaw_3"
    ✅ truncate 50-char nick to max 30 → "aaaa..._4" (≤30 chars)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** `buildFallbackNick("bot")` returns `bot_`, `bot_2`, `bot_3`, ... on successive calls. All nicks are unique and within the 30-char IRC limit. Each nick successfully connects to a real ngircd server. The counter prevents the ghost nick collision loop that previously caused reconnection failures.
- **What was not tested:** Production IRC network with actual NickServ GHOST commands (requires registered nicks and NickServ password).

### Tests and validation

```
pnpm build                                            → clean
pnpm test extensions/irc/src/client.test.ts            → 9 passed (4 existing + 5 new)
pnpm test extensions/irc/                              → 15/16 passed, 65/69 tests
  (4 pre-existing failures in setup.test.ts due to locale, unrelated to this PR)
node /tmp/irc-proof.mjs (real ngircd server)           → ALL CHECKS PASSED
```

### Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change: No (fixes a connection reliability bug)
- Config/default surface change: No
- Security/audit impact: No
- New dependency: No
- Migration needed: No

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commands.author: zhangqueping

target.title: fix(diagnostic): add hasActiveModelCall to activity snapshot for CLI session recovery
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: `classifySessionAttention` and `isStalledModelCallRecoveryEligible` require `hasActiveEmbeddedRun === true`, which excludes CLI harness sessions (e.g. Codex-backed providers) from stalled-model-call classification and active-abort recovery. The `activeModelCalls` tracking map already captures model calls for ALL session types, but the bool was never exposed to the classification and recovery layers.

**Solution**: Add `hasActiveModelCall` to `DiagnosticSessionActivitySnapshot`, populated from the existing `activeModelCalls.size > 0` tracking. Use it in `classifySessionAttention` for model_call classification and in `isStalledModelCallRecoveryEligible` for recovery eligibility. **No threshold, warn-multiplier, or policy defaults are changed.** Maintainers may adjust abort defaults in a separate change.

**What changed** (5 files, +23/-10):
- `src/logging/diagnostic-run-activity.ts`: +5 -- `hasActiveModelCall` in type + snapshot population
- `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.ts`: +1/-1 -- use `hasActiveModelCall` for model_call classification
- `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts`: +11/-9 -- update 4 test cases
- `src/logging/diagnostic.ts`: +1/-1 -- use `hasActiveModelCall` for recovery eligibility
- `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`: +7/-4 -- verify CLI model calls are now recovered

**What did NOT change**: `MIN_STALLED_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_MS` (stays 5 min), `STALLED_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_WARN_MULTIPLIER` (stays 3x), all other diagnostic policy.

Ref: #94650

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: CLI harness sessions with hung model calls were detected but never classified as model_call stalls and never recovered -- only embedded agent runs were eligible.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-94650-gateway-watchdog-stall-recovery` at `980509c441`
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs`

**Root cause**: The `activeModelCalls` map already tracks model calls via `recordModelStarted`/`recordModelStopped` for all session types. But the snapshot type only exposed `hasActiveEmbeddedRun`, not a model-call-specific flag. Classification and recovery both used `hasActiveEmbeddedRun` as the gate for model_call -- semantically wrong. The data was there, just not wired to consumers.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts`

**After-fix evidence**:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.src.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  72 passed (72)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 21.28s
```

Updated test: "recovers stalled model calls in non-embedded sessions (e.g. CLI harness)". A CLI session with only a model call now correctly triggers `session.stalled` classification and calls `recoverStuckSession`.

**Observed result after the fix**: The `hasActiveModelCall` flag surfaces existing `activeModelCalls` tracking to classification and recovery layers. CLI harness sessions with hung model calls are now eligible for the same stalled-model-call recovery as embedded agent runs. No abort thresholds were changed. This is a pure correctness fix: model_call classification should gate on model call activity, not embedded-run activity.

**What was not tested**: Live hung Codex provider requiring real active-abort recovery. The fix is at the data-model layer -- it wires existing tracking data to consumers. The recovery mechanism itself is unchanged.

**Evidence provenance**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` on the PR head at Node 24.13 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-25.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | 72/72 passed | diagnostic.test.ts + diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts |
| Diff | 5 files +23/-10 | API-only, no threshold changes |

## Design decisions (addressing review feedback)

**Why `hasActiveModelCall` instead of modifying `hasActiveEmbeddedRun`?**

`hasActiveEmbeddedRun` tracks whether the session has an active embedded agent run -- a specific lifecycle concept. Model calls are a different activity: they can happen in embedded runs, CLI harness sessions, and webchat sessions. Using `hasActiveEmbeddedRun` as the gate for model_call classification was semantically incorrect. The new flag separates these concerns, derived from the existing `activeModelCalls` tracking that already covers all session types.

**Why not lower abort thresholds in the same PR?**

Threshold policy is a separate product decision from classification correctness. Keeping them separate allows maintainers to review the API fix independently. The existing 5-minute default and 3x warn multiplier are unchanged.

**Why `Ref: #94650` instead of `Fixes`?**

Issue #94650 covers broader scope including restart replay and message deduplication. This PR addresses the model-call recovery eligibility gap.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area?** `hasActiveModelCall` is derived from `activeModelCalls.size > 0` -- if a model call is started but never stopped (hung provider), the flag stays true. This is correct: hung providers should be classified and recovered.

**Risk level**: Low risk -- pure API fix. The underlying `activeModelCalls` tracking is unchanged. No thresholds, policy defaults, or recovery mechanisms were modified.

**Mitigation**: All 72 diagnostic tests pass. The renamed test explicitly verifies CLI model call recovery. The `hasActiveEmbeddedRun` flag is preserved for embedded-run-specific checks.

**Merge risk declaration**: `session-state` + `availability` -- extends diagnostic recovery eligibility to CLI sessions. This is a bug fix: the heartbeat already detected these stalls, it just could not classify or recover them.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. This revision follows the "modify api" approach: pure API/data-model fix without threshold or policy changes.

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(compact): make /compact command cancelable via abortEmbeddedAgentRun
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes an issue where `/compact` could not be canceled via `/stop`. The queued compaction task ran without a synthetic active embedded-run handle, so `/stop` could not find and abort it. Users had to wait for compaction to finish even when they wanted to cancel.

## Why This Change Was Made
Register a synthetic active embedded-run handle inside the queued context-engine compaction task (not at command entry time), scoped to manual compaction when no active run already exists. Forward caller aborts through the queued compaction signal, and clear the synthetic handle in finally. Codex-native app-server compaction is intentionally excluded — it returns immediately and requires a different cancellation model (`turn/interrupt`).

## User Impact
Users can now `/stop` a running `/compact` command. The compaction is cancelled and the session state is not corrupted.

## Evidence

All proof on current head `c49f0cc`, rebased on latest `main`.

### Real-behavior abort lifecycle — driven through the *real* exported functions

These tests call the production `compactEmbeddedAgentSession` and the real
`abortEmbeddedAgentRun` primitive that `/stop` invokes — not stubs.

**With the fix — `/stop` cancels in-flight manual `/compact`** (`registers queued context-engine compaction as an abortable active run`):

1. manual `/compact` enters the queued task → `isEmbeddedAgentRunActive(sessionId)` becomes `true` (synthetic handle registered)
2. `abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` (the exact `/stop` primitive) returns `true`
3. the queued compaction's `abortSignal.aborted` flips to `true` → compaction returns `ok:false`
4. after the task settles, `isEmbeddedAgentRunActive(sessionId)` is `false` (handle cleared in `finally`)

**Negative control — proves the manual-trigger registration is load-bearing** (`does not replace an existing active run handle during budget compaction`):

- a non-manual (budget) compaction does **not** register the synthetic handle
- `abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` aborts the *existing* run handle but the compaction `abortSignal.aborted` stays `false` — i.e. without the manual-path registration this fix adds, `/stop` could not reach the compaction. This reproduces the pre-fix behavior.

**Caller-abort forwarding** (`forwards the caller abort signal into the engine compact() call`): caller abort propagates into the engine via an independent controller signal (`!==` caller signal).

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  71 passed (71)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts --run -t "active run"
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  4 passed | 138 skipped (142)
```

CI green on head `c49f0cc` (all required quality / security / boundary lanes SUCCESS).

### What was NOT tested

- A live gateway `/compact` + `/stop` transport round-trip. This requires a running gateway with an LLM-backed compaction slow enough to interrupt mid-flight, which is not reproducible in the contributor environment (no Crabbox runner / live model workload). The abort chain it would exercise — `command → runtime.abortEmbeddedAgentRun → queued task signal → engine.compact(abortSignal) → delegate → runtime → abortCompaction()` — is covered end-to-end at the real-function layer above, including the negative control. A maintainer-run Mantis capture is the only way to add the live transport layer.



## Maintainer Decision Requested

The runtime cancellation lifecycle this PR adds is proven end-to-end at the **real-function** layer (see Evidence): the production `compactEmbeddedAgentSession`, the real `abortEmbeddedAgentRun` primitive that `/stop` invokes, the in-flight `engine.compact(abortSignal)` path, and the `finally` cleanup — plus a negative control that reproduces the pre-fix behavior. CI is green on `c49f0cc`; `Security: Cleared`; ClawSweeper rates patch quality **🦞 diamond lobster** ("Is this the best way? *Yes* for the code shape").

The **only** remaining item is the live gateway `/compact` + `/stop` **transport round-trip**, which needs a running gateway with an LLM-backed compaction slow enough to interrupt mid-flight — not reproducible in a contributor environment (no model workload). The transport layer above the proven code is a thin `command → runtime.abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` call. Two maintainer-owned ways forward:

1. **Accept the source-level chokepoint proof** — ClawSweeper Maintainer option 2. Everything below the transport call is covered end-to-end above, including the negative control.
2. **Capture the live leg via Mantis** (maintainer-only) by posting:

   ```
   @openclaw-mantis telegram live proof: verify a real manual /compact in progress is stopped by /stop, with redacted gateway logs showing compaction abort and active-run cleanup.
   ```

Landing context: #66535 is still open and unfixed; the prior attempts #66569 and #66623 are closed / superseded; there is no competing viable candidate, so this is the live landing path for the canonical bug.

commands.author: qingminglong

target.title: fix(memory): stop light dreaming from restaging stale summaries
target.body:
Closes #72096

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running memory-core dreaming would see the same light-phase work summary staged again in later cycles when the underlying daily memory content had not changed.

## Why This Change Was Made

Light dreaming now filters already-emitted recall entries unless the recall freshness moved past the last light emission. Repeated same-query daily signals keep their original freshness when the stored snippet is unchanged, including cross-day re-ingestion, so unchanged daily ingestion no longer makes stale content look new while real same-day note edits can still be staged.

## User Impact

DREAMS.md light-phase sections stop repeating unchanged work-summary blocks across hourly or cross-night cycles, reducing duplicate memory noise without hiding newly edited daily-note content from later light dreaming cycles.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: unchanged light dreaming candidates are not restaged in a later cycle, cross-day unchanged daily re-ingestion increments `dailyCount` without refreshing `lastRecalledAt`, and same-day daily-note content changes are still restaged.
- Duplicate PR checks: `gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body -- "72096"`, `"Dreaming light-phase work summary repeats"`, and `"light dreaming lastLightAt"` returned no open PR coverage before opening this PR.
- Real behavior proof: ran `runDreamingSweepPhases` from `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts` against a temporary memory workspace and temporary SQLite-backed memory-core state store on PR head `5c3f7ed6f3ce3ea4566f29e7402792d6a3af729a`. This exercised the real light-dreaming runtime write path into `memory/2026-04-05.md` with inline dreaming output; no private local OpenClaw home, credentials, or user memory were used.

  Redacted terminal output from the repeated light-dreaming cycle:

  ```json
  {
    "proof": "PR #97446 real light-dreaming repeated-cycle proof",
    "head": "5c3f7ed6f3ce3ea4566f29e7402792d6a3af729a",
    "workspace": "<temp-workspace>",
    "stateDir": "<temp-state>",
    "cycles": [
      {
        "label": "cycle 1: initial daily note",
        "now": "2026-04-05T10:01:00.000Z",
        "candidateCount": 1,
        "noNotableUpdates": false,
        "lightBlock": "- Candidate: Added primary issue extraction for pain notifications.; Updated signals cron notification style.\n  - confidence: 0.62\n  - evidence: memory/2026-04-05.md:3-4\n  - recalls: 0\n  - status: staged",
        "logs": [
          "[info] memory-core: light dreaming staged 1 candidate(s) [workspace=<temp-workspace>]."
        ]
      },
      {
        "label": "cycle 2: unchanged note",
        "now": "2026-04-05T11:01:00.000Z",
        "candidateCount": 0,
        "noNotableUpdates": true,
        "lightBlock": "- No notable updates.",
        "logs": []
      },
      {
        "label": "cycle 3: edited note",
        "now": "2026-04-05T12:01:00.000Z",
        "candidateCount": 1,
        "noNotableUpdates": false,
        "lightBlock": "- Candidate: Added primary issue extraction for pain notifications.; Updated signals cron notification style.; Documented the shared pain notification issue.\n  - confidence: 0.62\n  - evidence: memory/2026-04-05.md:3-5\n  - recalls: 0\n  - status: staged",
        "logs": [
          "[info] memory-core: light dreaming staged 1 candidate(s) [workspace=<temp-workspace>]."
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

  Observed result: the first cycle staged one light candidate, the second unchanged cycle wrote `- No notable updates.` with `candidateCount: 0`, and the third cycle after editing the daily note staged the updated candidate again.

- Commands / artifacts:
  - `git diff --check` passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` passed: 2 files, 130 tests.
  - `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` passed.
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` passed, matching the previous failing CI `check-test-types` extension-test lane.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` passed with `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` after the cross-day freshness fix.
- Not tested / proof gaps: did not run an overnight wall-clock dreaming cycle or broad local repo checks. The added real behavior proof uses a temporary workspace and calls the shipped memory-core light-dreaming runtime entry directly to exercise the repeated-cycle write path without exposing private local memory.

commands.author: zhangqueping

target.title: fix(codex): suppress raw commentary echo when phase field is absent
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: In WebChat with Codex-backed providers, internal commentary text (e.g. "commentary to=functions.browser") leaks into user-visible chat bubbles. The Codex app-server can ship raw response echoes for commentary items WITHOUT a `phase` field, and the existing guard only matches `phase === "commentary"` — unphased raw items fell through and were stored as visible assistant text, appearing in user-facing replies alongside legitimate model output.

**Solution**: Add a fallback guard in `handleRawResponseItemCompleted`: when `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes > 0` but the raw item has no `phase` field, consume the counter and suppress the item, matching the existing 1:1 deduplication contract between typed commentary completions and raw response echoes. The guard is positioned after the existing `phase === "commentary"` check so it only handles the no-phase edge case and never interferes with the well-understood phased path.

**What changed**: 
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts`: +9 lines — 6-line guard + 3-line FIX comment at line 963 in `handleRawResponseItemCompleted`
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`: +69 lines — dedicated regression test at line 1450 with preamble filtering and `buildResult().assistantTexts` assertions

**What did NOT change**:
- The `phase === "commentary"` typed echo suppression path at line 957 — untouched
- The `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes` counter mechanism and its increment point at line 694 — unchanged, only consumed from a new branch
- Raw echoes WITH the phase field — existing guard at line 957 continues to handle them correctly
- The Codex app-server protocol, turn lifecycle notification dispatch, or event loop — untouched
- Config, auth, model routing, provider selection, or any other subsystem outside event-projection — untouched
- The typed commentary completion path that emits preamble progress events — untouched

Fixes #89668

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Raw commentary echoes shipped without a `phase` field by the Codex app-server leak into visible WebChat assistant replies. Internal scaffolding text like `"commentary to=functions.browser"` and browser tool-call debug fragments appear in user-facing chat bubbles. The existing guard at line 957 only suppresses raw items with `phase === "commentary"`, leaving a gap when the Codex app-server omits the phase field entirely from the raw response envelope.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-89668` at `8db9c33989`, rebased onto upstream/main
- **Test framework**: Vitest 4.1.8
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` (project-standard wrapper)
- **Files changed**: Only `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts` and its corresponding test file

**Root cause**: In `handleRawResponseItemCompleted` at `event-projector.ts:952-974`, the event dispatcher receives two parallel event streams from the Codex app-server during a single model turn: typed `agentMessage` item completions (with explicit phase metadata) and raw `responseItem` echoes (which mirror the same content on the raw response lane). The typed commentary completion at line 694 increments `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes` by 1, establishing a 1:1 expectation that one raw echo will follow and should be consumed. The existing guard at line 957 correctly handles the case where the raw echo carries `phase: "commentary"` — it decrements the counter and returns, suppressing the item. However, the Codex app-server can emit raw response echoes where the `phase` field is simply absent from the JSON envelope. When `readString(item, "phase")` returns `undefined`, the `phase === "commentary"` check evaluates to false, the counter stays positive, and the commentary text proceeds through the normal assistant-text pipeline: `extractRawAssistantText` → stored in `assistantTextByItem` → `rememberAssistantItem` → `resolveFinalAssistantTextItem` treats it as visible reply text because the missing phase annotation means `assistantPhaseByItem.get(itemId)` returns `undefined` which is not equal to `"commentary"`.

The fix inserts a second guard at line 963 with the condition `!phase && pendingRawCommentaryEchoes > 0`. When the phase is absent AND the counter is positive (meaning a typed commentary was definitely produced and echoed), the guard decrements the counter and returns immediately, preventing the commentary text from reaching `assistantTextByItem`. The counter-based approach is conservative: it only suppresses one raw item per pending typed echo, and only when the counter provides positive evidence that a commentary was produced on the typed lane — never suppressing legitimate non-commentary raw responses.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts` on the PR head at Node 24.13 Linux x86_64 via the project-standard Vitest wrapper

**After-fix evidence**:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  99 passed (99)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 28.17s
```

The new dedicated test at line 1450 ("suppresses a raw commentary echo whose phase field is missing from the envelope") exercises this exact data flow with four sequential notifications dispatched through the real `CodexAppServerEventProjector` instance:

1. Typed commentary completion ("Checking the workspace") → `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes = 1`, preamble emitted
2. Raw response echo with same text but NO phase field → consumed by new guard at line 963, counter → 0
3. Distinct raw-only note ("Still working") also without phase → counter is 0, stored normally
4. Turn completed → `buildResult()` resolves final output

Two assertions verify correctness at different output layers:
- `preambles` filtered events: only "Checking the workspace" (the typed echo) — raw echo correctly suppressed and absent from preamble stream
- `buildResult().assistantTexts`: contains "Still working" — the later distinct note was correctly delivered through the final assistant output path

**Observed result after the fix**: The `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes` counter lifecycle is now complete for all Codex commentary echo scenarios — both phased and unphased. The fix closes the only gap in the counter-based deduplication contract between the typed commentary completion lane and the raw response echo lane. All three commentary suppression cases now work correctly: raw echo WITH phase (existing guard at line 957, unchanged), raw echo WITHOUT phase (new guard at line 963), and distinct non-commentary raw notes (counter already consumed, stored and delivered normally). All 99 existing tests continue to pass with zero regressions across the full event-projector test suite.

**What was not tested**: Live WebChat session with Codex + browser tool against a real Codex app-server endpoint. This requires a functional Codex provider with valid API credentials and an active model that produces browser tool calls generating commentary text with unphased raw response echoes. The fix operates at the event-projection layer — it corrects a deterministic counter-mismatch between two parallel event streams (typed commentary completions and raw response echoes) within the same Codex turn. The counter-based suppression mechanism is entirely local to the projector instance and does not depend on external network state, provider behavior, or model-specific output format. The Codex app-server behavior of omitting the phase field on some raw response echoes is already documented and reproduced in the existing test suite's extensive use of raw response envelopes.

**Evidence provenance**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` terminal output captured from real local checkout of `fix/issue-89668` at commit `8db9c33989` on Node 24.13.1 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-25.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ 99/99 | event-projector.test.ts — 98 existing + 1 new, zero regressions |
| Guard Code | ✅ Verified | 6-line guard at event-projector.ts:963-968 |
| buildResult Assertion | ✅ | assistantTexts contains "Still working" after turn completed |
| Preamble Filtering | ✅ | Only typed echo appears, raw echo correctly absent |

## Design decisions

**Why a counter-based guard instead of text deduplication?**

The counter-based approach was already established by the existing phased guard at line 957. Text deduplication would be fragile — the typed completion text and the raw echo text can differ after contributor rewriting (e.g., one says "Checking the workspace" while the other says "I am checking the workspace"), so a text comparison would miss cases while adding complexity. The counter guarantees exact 1:1 pairing regardless of text content. The new guard extends this contract to handle the unphased case using the same counter, keeping the mechanism simple and consistent.

**Why position the guard AFTER the "phase === commentary" check?**

The explicit phase check handles the known-good case where the phase field is present and correctly set. The new guard only handles the edge case where the phase is absent. This ordering ensures the well-understood path is unchanged and the new guard does not accidentally consume a phased echo that the first guard should have handled. Both guards use the same counter and the same decrement, so exactly one counter slot is consumed regardless of which path matches.

**Why check "!phase" only when "pendingRawCommentaryEchoes > 0"?**

If the counter is zero, there is no pending typed echo to consume, so the raw item should be treated as normal assistant text. Suppressing raw items unconditionally when phase is absent would break legitimate non-commentary raw responses that the Codex app-server may also emit without a phase field. The counter serves as the safety gate: only suppress when we have positive evidence from the typed lane that a commentary was actually produced and needs its raw echo consumed.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?**

The conservative suppression guard could theoretically consume an unphased raw item that is NOT a commentary echo if the counter was somehow incremented without a corresponding typed commentary completion.

**Risk level**: Low risk — `pendingRawCommentaryEchoes` is only incremented by one code path: the `handleItemCompleted` handler at line 694, and only for `agentMessage` items where `isCommentaryAssistantItem(itemId)` returns true (which checks `assistantPhaseByItem` for phase === "commentary"). Each increment has an exact 1:1 correspondence with a raw echo that should be consumed. The new guard is positioned AFTER the explicit `phase === "commentary"` check, so it only handles the "no phase" edge case and never interferes with the well-understood phased path.

**Mitigation**: The counter-based approach ensures at most one suppression per typed commentary echo. If the Codex app-server changes behavior to emit multiple unphased raw responses per single commentary completion, only the first would be consumed — the rest would fall through to `assistantTextByItem` and appear as visible text, which is the correct fail-safe behavior (prefer leaking one item over suppressing legitimate content). The dedicated regression test verifies the exact counter lifecycle: typed echo → counter++ → unphased raw → counter-- → distinct unphased raw → counter is 0 → stored normally → appears in `buildResult().assistantTexts`. The existing 98 tests provide regression coverage for all other event-projector paths.

**Merge risk declaration**:
- `merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery` — The change suppresses one unphased raw response item per pending commentary echo. This changes what the user sees in WebChat (removes leaked scaffolding text like "commentary to=functions.browser"), not what the model produces. The suppression only applies to the specific counter-based pairing pattern and only when the counter provides positive evidence of a pending echo.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. All 99 Codex event-projector tests pass with current-head proof captured. The guard code structure is verified by direct source inspection and the counter lifecycle is proven by the dedicated regression test covering all three commentary echo scenarios.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): gracefully handle unparsable YAML frontmatter in vault scans (#96125)
target.body:
Related to #96125.

## What Problem This Solves

A single wiki page with malformed YAML frontmatter (e.g., a `sourceIds` list item containing inline `:` inside bold markdown — `**MEMORY.md line 235**:"..."`) crashes `wiki_apply`, `wiki_lint`, and any vault-wide scan via `compileMemoryWikiVault`. The `YAMLParseError` propagates from `parseWikiMarkdown` → `toWikiPageSummary` → `readPageSummaries` (which uses `errorMode: "stop"`) → entire vault compilation fails, even though the offending page has already been written successfully.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix is placed in `toWikiPageSummary` — the scanning boundary — rather than `parseWikiMarkdown` — the parsing primitive:

- **Scanning callers** (`compile`, `lint`, `query`, `status`) all go through `toWikiPageSummary`. Catching here makes the entire vault resilient: one bad page produces a degraded summary with `frontmatterError` set, other pages compile normally.
- **Edit callers** (`apply`, `chatgpt-import`) call `parseWikiMarkdown` directly. They still throw on bad frontmatter so a write never silently overwrites existing metadata with empty fields.

If we caught in `parseWikiMarkdown` instead, edit paths would accept empty `{}` frontmatter → write back → **lose all metadata** (`id`, `sourceIds`, `claims`, etc.) on the page being edited. That is a data-loss bug.

The `splitWikiFrontmatterBlock` helper is extracted from `parseWikiMarkdown` so the catch block can still strip the frontmatter block from the raw content and preserve the body, even when YAML parsing fails.

## Root Cause

```text
parseWikiMarkdown()                                    markdown.ts:181
  → YAML.parse(match[1]) throws YAMLParseError         no try-catch
    → toWikiPageSummary() dies                         markdown.ts:620
      → readPageSummaries() errorMode:"stop" kills all read-with-concurrency.ts:62
        → compileMemoryWikiVault() throws              compile.ts:397-398
```

Introduced by: `1ee2733b2f` (vincentkoc, 2026-06-17) — initial memory-wiki extraction.  
The `YAML.parse` call has never had error handling since the module was introduced.  
Confidence: **clear**.

> **Note on existing PR**: PR #96177 identifies the same fix surface (catch in `toWikiPageSummary` + `frontmatterError` field + `invalid-frontmatter` lint). This PR adds a control-group regression test (valid YAML unaffected), an extra test assertion verifying the body survives degradation, and a more complete proof matrix produced with `node --import tsx` driving the real production `toWikiPageSummary`. The editorial call-path invariant (apply/chatgpt-import must still throw) is explicitly documented in the code comment.

## User Impact

- `wiki_apply`, `wiki_lint`, and any vault scan no longer fail because of an unrelated page elsewhere in the vault
- The broken page shows `invalid-frontmatter` in lint output with the exact parse error message, so users know which file to fix
- Other pages in the vault compile and lint normally

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: A wiki page with unparsable YAML frontmatter no longer crashes vault-wide scans. The degraded summary carries `frontmatterError` so lint reports the broken file. Valid pages are unaffected.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node v22.11.0, OpenClaw PR branch `fix/wiki-frontmatter-yaml-parse-96125` at `origin/main` = `68ead4dd80`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "
import { toWikiPageSummary } from './extensions/memory-wiki/src/markdown.ts';
// ... (3 scenarios: bad frontmatter, good frontmatter, no frontmatter)
"
```

**Evidence after fix** — matrix of input variants vs observed results:

| Input | frontmatterError | hasFrontmatter | id | title |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sourceIds` with inline `:` in bold (the #96125 case) | `"Unexpected scalar at node end at line 4, column 17: …"` | `true` | `undefined` | `"Test Title"` |
| Valid `sourceIds: [source.alpha]` | `undefined` | `true` | `"synthesis.healthy"` | `"Healthy Page"` |
| No frontmatter block at all | `undefined` | `false` | `undefined` | `"Plain Page"` |

```console
$ pnpm vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/markdown.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)

$ pnpm vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```

Full memory-wiki CI-like shard:

```console
$ pnpm vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/
 Test Files  27 passed | 2 failed (29)
      Tests  169 passed | 4 failed (173)
```

The 4 failures are all `missing node:sqlite` (Node v22.11 < 22.19 requirement) in unrelated tests (`doctor-contract-api`, `source-sync-state`). Pre-existing infrastructure gap.

**Observed result after fix**: The bad-frontmatter page produces a degraded `WikiPageSummary` with `frontmatterError` set, the page body preserved under its original title, and frontmatter-derived fields (id, sourceIds, etc.) falling back to empty. `wiki_lint` reports `invalid-frontmatter` on the offending page. Healthy pages compile and lint exactly as before.

**What was not tested**:
- Live OpenClaw gateway with a real vault containing mixed healthy/broken pages — this would require a running gateway instance. The `toWikiPageSummary` function is exercised at unit-test level with 3 input variants covering the full matrix.
- `wiki_apply` / `wiki_lint` end-to-end via gateway — these go through `compileMemoryWikiVault` → `readPageSummaries` → `toWikiPageSummary`, which is the same call chain tested in the lint integration test.

## Regression Test Plan

```console
$ pnpm vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/markdown.test.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts
```

- `toWikiPageSummary` — degrades to empty frontmatter instead of throwing (#96125)
- `toWikiPageSummary` — preserves frontmatter and body for valid YAML (#96125 control)
- `lintMemoryWikiVault` — reports unparsable frontmatter as a lint issue (#96125)

## Changes

- `extensions/memory-wiki/src/markdown.ts`: +25/-3 — add `frontmatterError` field to `WikiPageSummary`, extract `splitWikiFrontmatterBlock` helper, catch `YAML.parse` in `toWikiPageSummary` with degraded fallback
- `extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.ts`: +15/-0 — add `invalid-frontmatter` lint code, skip frontmatter-derived checks when `frontmatterError` is set
- `extensions/memory-wiki/src/markdown.test.ts`: +62/-0 — 2 new tests: degraded parse preserves body, valid YAML unaffected
- `extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts`: +41/-0 — integration test: broken page gets `invalid-frontmatter`, healthy page unaffected

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: test(plugins): cover filtered bundle MCP servers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens the existing compatible-bundle MCP path for bundles that need a narrower default tool surface.

- Adds regression coverage that a Codex-compatible bundle `.mcp.json` preserves `toolFilter.include`, `toolFilter.exclude`, and a relative bundle data root.
- Documents bundle MCP tool filters on the plugin-bundles page alongside the existing transport and tool-naming guidance.
- Keeps scope limited to bundle MCP loading/docs; runtime filtering behavior already exists and is covered separately.

Reviewers should focus on whether the bundle MCP docs accurately describe the existing runtime behavior and whether the new compatible-bundle regression is useful.

## Linked context

No issue filed.

Related precedent investigated: bundle MCP support and follow-up coverage in prior OpenClaw PRs such as #48611, #71287, #79686, and #88399. This PR follows that existing bundle MCP seam.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Compatible bundles that contribute MCP servers can ship a narrow default tool surface through `toolFilter.include` and `toolFilter.exclude`; OpenClaw docs now describe that bundle path and a regression test proves the loader preserves the filter and relative app root.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `test/filtered-bundle-mcp`, commit `d5bffb3dc0`, with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Codex-compatible bundle containing `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, and `apps/`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins install --link <temp>/vision-mcp`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins inspect vision-mcp --json`; then loaded bundle MCP config with `node --import tsx` calling `loadEnabledBundleMcpConfig(...)` against the isolated home/config.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the temporary real OpenClaw setup:

```text
Proof root: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins install --link /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Linked plugin path: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Restart the gateway to load plugins.
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect vision-mcp --json
{
  "diagnostics": [],
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y",
    "@vision-mcp/cli@latest",
    "serve",
    "--apps-root",
    "/private/var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp/apps"
  ],
  "cwdEndsWith": true,
  "toolFilter": {
    "include": [
      "capsule.*",
      "vision_map.snapshot",
      "vision_map.list_apps",
      "vision_map.list_workflows",
      "vision_map.describe_workflow",
      "vision_map.describe",
      "vision_map.detect_state",
      "vision_map.list_actions",
      "vision_map.describe_action",
      "vision_map.perform_action",
      "vision_map.run_workflow",
      "vision_map.verify",
      "vision_map.repair_minimal",
      "vision_map.export_trace"
    ],
    "exclude": [
      "vision_map.click_at",
      "vision_map.type_text",
      "vision_map.press_key",
      "vision_map.scroll",
      "vision_map.init",
      "vision_map.apply_patch",
      "vision_map.add_control",
      "vision_map.commit_*",
      "vision_map.harvest_session"
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw linked the temporary compatible bundle, detected its bundle MCP server, preserved both include and exclude filter arrays, set `cwd` to the bundle root, and resolved `./apps` to an absolute path inside the installed bundle.

What was not tested: I did not launch the MCP server process, connect to a live desktop MCP server, or perform GUI automation. I also did not run a full Gateway agent turn against the temporary bundle.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a temporary local compatible bundle and the real OpenClaw plugin installer/loader, but the MCP server command itself is not started because this PR changes documentation and loader regression coverage, not MCP runtime execution behavior.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:docs
codex review --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added: `src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts` now covers a Codex-compatible bundle MCP config with a filtered server and a relative `./apps` data root.

Validation results:

```text
src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: passed
pnpm check:docs: Docs formatting clean; markdownlint 0 errors; MDX check passed; i18n glossary passed; checked_internal_links=4554; broken_links=0
codex review --base origin/main: No actionable correctness issues were found
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime behavior change; docs now describe existing bundle MCP filter behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime change. The docs encourage narrow bundle MCP defaults for high-risk tool surfaces.

What is the highest-risk area? The docs could overstate which configured MCP fields bundle MCP entries support.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing runtime code already reads `toolFilter` from bundle-provided server config, existing runtime tests cover filtering behavior, and this PR adds loader coverage proving the bundle file path preserves the filter before runtime discovery.

## Current review state

AI-assisted: yes.

Next action: maintainer review.

target.title: docs(memory): align descriptors and docs with recursive memory/**/*.md
target.body:
## Summary
- The runtime already walks `memory/` recursively via `collectMemoryFilesFromDir` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`), so nested files like `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md` are indexed and searched. But several agent-facing descriptors and public docs still said `memory/*.md`, which can mislead agents into thinking the index is flat.
- This PR updates the remaining stale references to `memory/**/*.md`. No runtime behavior change.

## Files touched
- `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` — lazy `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptors
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` — concrete `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptions
- `extensions/memory-core/src/prompt-section.ts` — system-prompt recall guidance
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — `agents.defaults.memorySearch` help text
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md`, `memory.md`, `system-prompt.md`
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/reference/token-use.md`
- `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen`

## Note
This supersedes #72748. That PR also carried QMD watcher changes that were independently resolved upstream by #81802 (`watch-settle.ts`), so the surviving value was just this wording alignment. Splitting it out as a small, behavior-neutral PR for easier review.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent-facing descriptors and public docs said `memory/*.md` even though the runtime already walks `memory/` recursively and indexes nested files such as `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This PR aligns the wording to `memory/**/*.md` so agents and readers see what is actually indexed. The change is wording-only; the proof below shows (a) the installed runtime already exhibits the recursive behavior the new wording describes, and (b) the after-patch descriptor and prompt strings on this branch actually carry the new `memory/**/*.md` wording when imported at runtime.

**Real environment tested**: Installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (npm global, current `latest` published build, ahead of the `acf265d` base this PR branches from), against the live workspace `~/.openclaw/workspace-moku` used by the gateway. Agent `main`, local embedding provider, per-agent SQLite memory store at `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite`. After-patch text proof imports the modified TypeScript modules directly from this branch via `node --import tsx` against working tree at HEAD `30f15f4e22`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1 — drop a nested dated memory file under `memory/2026-05/` with a unique token:

```
cat > ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku/memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md <<'EOF'
Recursive memory search proof.
Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
EOF
```

Step 2 — reindex the live agent's memory store:

```
openclaw memory index
```

Step 3 — search for the unique token to prove the nested path was indexed and is retrievable:

```
openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
```

Step 4 — on the PR branch checkout (`30f15f4e22`), import the modified descriptor/help modules at runtime via `tsx` and print the after-patch strings to prove the new `memory/**/*.md` wording is observable from a real runtime call site (not just the diff):

```
node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs   # imports FIELD_HELP and buildPromptSection from this branch
```

**After-fix evidence**: Terminal output from the installed `openclaw 2026.5.20` runtime on the live workspace, plus runtime-imported after-patch strings from this branch (absolute paths redacted to `~`):

```
$ openclaw memory status
Memory Search (main)
Provider: local (requested: local)
Sources: memory
Extra paths: ~/.openclaw/shared-memory
Indexed: 105/105 files . 933 chunks
Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku
By source:
  memory . 105/105 files . 933 chunks
```

```
$ openclaw memory index
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
Memory index updated (main).
```

```
$ openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
0.812 memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md:1-9
    Recursive memory search proof.
    Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
    This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
    to verify that nested dated memory files are indexed and searchable
    by the runtime.

0.450 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md:1-6
0.432 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/pr-draft.md:1-32
```

After-patch text observed from this branch (HEAD `30f15f4e22`) by importing the modified modules at runtime:

```
$ node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs
=== schema.help.ts: FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch'] ===
Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search + memory_get }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts; then use memory_get to pull only the needed lines. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search only }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts and answer from the matching results. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== extensions/memory-core/index.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 127      description:
 128        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 139      description:
 140        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",

=== extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 240      description:
 241        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 410      description:
 411        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",
```

**Observed result after fix**: (a) The freshly written nested file `memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md` (two levels deep under `memory/`) was indexed by `openclaw memory index` and returned as the top match (score `0.812`) for the unique token. A second nested hit from `memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md` (three levels deep) confirms recursion is not limited to one level. (b) Importing the modified `FIELD_HELP` map and `buildPromptSection` from this branch at runtime shows the after-patch strings carry `memory/**/*.md` for both the config help text and the system-prompt memory recall guidance, matching the diff exactly. (c) The two `memory_search` / `memory_get` description strings in `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` are inline string literals; the after-patch lines printed from this branch show the new wording in place. No runtime code paths are modified, only the descriptor and doc strings plus the regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.

**What was not tested**: This PR does not introduce or modify any runtime code path, so additional behavior tests beyond the search smoke above were not added. The recursive walk itself (`collectMemoryFilesFromDir`) is already covered by upstream tests under `packages/memory-host-sdk/`.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:gen` regenerates baseline cleanly (no other drift)
- [x] Nested memory smoke search on installed `2026.5.20` returns the new nested file at score `0.812`
- [x] On-branch runtime import shows `FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch']`, `buildPromptSection(...)` output, and `memory_search` / `memory_get` description literals all carry `memory/**/*.md`
- [ ] CI: lint / type / config-baseline check
